(c)(3). , amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) read as follows: “Any nonprofit institution, or group thereof, or consortia of nonprofit institutions, including entities existing on , may submit to the Secretary an application for financial support under this subsection, in accordance with the procedures established by the Secretary and published in the Federal Register under paragraph (2). In order to receive assistance under this section, an applicant shall provide adequate assurances that it will contribute 50 percent or more of the proposed Center’s capital and annual operating and maintenance costs for the first three years and an increasing share for each of the last three years. Each applicant shall also submit a proposal for the allocation of the legal rights associated with any invention which may result from the proposed Center’s activities.”Pub. L. 110–69, § 3003(a)August 23, 1988Subsec. (c)(5). , inserted “A Center that has not received a positive evaluation by the evaluation panel shall be notified by the panel of the deficiencies in its performance and shall be placed on probation for one year, after which time the panel shall reevaluate the Center. If the Center has not addressed the deficiencies identified by the panel, or shown a significant improvement in its performance, the Director shall conduct a new competition to select an operator for the Center or may close the Center.” after “at declining levels.”Pub. L. 110–69, § 3003(b)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d) and struck out former subsec. (d). Text of former subsec. (d) read as follows: “In addition to such sums as may be authorized and appropriated to the Secretary and Director to operate the Centers program, the Secretary and Director also may accept funds from other Federal departments and agencies for the purpose of providing Federal funds to support Centers. Any Center which is supported with funds which originally came from other Federal departments and agencies shall be selected and operated according to the provisions of this section.”Pub. L. 110–69, § 3003(c)Subsec. (e). , added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 110–69, § 3003(d)Subsec. (f). , added subsec. (f).Pub. L. 110–69, § 3003(e)1998—Subsec. (c)(5). substituted “. After the sixth year, a Center may receive additional financial support under this section if it has received a positive evaluation through an independent review, under procedures established by the Institute. Such an independent review shall be required at least every two years after the sixth year of operation. Funding received for a fiscal year under this section after the sixth year of operation shall not exceed one third of the capital and annual operating and maintenance costs of the Center under the program.” for “, which are designed to ensure that the Center no longer needs financial support from the Institute by the seventh year. In no event shall funding for a Center be provided by the Department of Commerce after the sixth year of the operation of a Center.”Pub. L. 105–3091992—Subsec. (c)(6). , inserted before period at end “except for contracts for such specific technology extension or transfer services as may be specified by statute or by the Director”.Pub. L. 102–245, § 105(e)(1)Subsec. (d). , amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. Such sums shall remain available until expended.”Pub. L. 102–245, § 105(e)(2)

Change of Name, , , which in part renamed the Manufacturing Extension Partnership Program authorized under this section as the Hollings Manufacturing Partnership Program and which named the centers established and receiving funding under former subsec. (a) of this section the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Centers, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 108–447, div. B, title II118 Stat. 2879Pub. L. 111–358, title IV, § 404(f)(2)124 Stat. 4002Dec. 8, 2004Jan. 4, 2011

Savings Provisions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–329, title V, § 501(f)130 Stat. 3033Jan. 6, 2017“Notwithstanding the amendments made by subsections (a) [sic; subsec. (a) is set out as a Short Title of 2017 Amendment note under ] and (b) of this section [amending this section], the Secretary of Commerce may carry out section 25 of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act () as that section was in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this Act [], with respect to existing grants, agreements, cooperative agreements, or contracts, and with respect to applications for such items that are received by the Secretary prior to the date of enactment of this Act.”section 271 of this title15 U.S.C. 278kJan. 6, 2017

Resource Optimization, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–167, div. B, title II, § 10251(b)136 Stat. 1498Aug. 9, 2022“Of amounts authorized for the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership program under section 25 of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act (), the Secretary [of Commerce] shall optimize funding across sections 25 and 25A of such Act [, 278k–1], as well as the program established under section 25B of such Act [] (as added by subsection (a)), to the extent practicable and subject to the availability of appropriations, in order to maximize Center (as such term is defined in such section 25) participation as well as competitiveness, productivity, and technological performance in United States manufacturing.”15 U.S.C. 278k15 U.S.C. 278k15 U.S.C. 278k–2

Patent Rights, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–329, title V, § 501(g)130 Stat. 3033Jan. 6, 2017“The provisions of chapter 18 of title 35, United States Code, shall apply, to the extent not inconsistent with section 25 of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act () and section 25 [sic] of that Act, to the promotion of technology from research by Centers under those sections, except for contracts for such specific technology extension or transfer services as may be specified by the Director of NIST [National Institute of Standards and Technology] or under other law.”15 U.S.C. 278k

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–358, title VII, § 702124 Stat. 4041Jan. 4, 2011“Congress finds the following:“(1)Over its 20-year existence, the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership has proven its value to manufacturers as demonstrated by the resulting impact on jobs and the economies of all 50 States and the Nation as a whole.“(2)The Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership has helped thousands of companies reinvest in themselves through process improvement and business growth initiatives leading to more sales, new markets, and the adoption of technology to deliver new products and services.“(3)Manufacturing is an increasingly important part of the construction sector as the industry moves to the use of more components and factory built sub-assemblies.“(4)Construction practices must become more efficient and precise if the United States is to construct and renovate its building stock to reduce related carbon emissions to levels that are consistent with combating global warming.“(5)Many companies involved in construction are small, without access to innovative manufacturing techniques, and could benefit from the type of training and business analysis activities that the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership routinely provides to the Nation’s manufacturers and their supply chains.“(6)Broadening the competitiveness grant program under [former] section 25(f) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act ([former] , now see ) could help develop and diffuse knowledge necessary to capture a large portion of the estimated $100 billion or more in energy savings if buildings in the United States met the level and quality of energy efficiency now found in buildings in certain other countries.15 U.S.C. 278k(f)15 U.S.C. 278k–1“(7)It is therefore in the national interest to expand the capabilities of the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership to be supportive of the construction and green energy industries.”

Agreements and Contributions for Collective Research and Development Initiatives, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 108–7, div. B, title II117 Stat. 73Feb. 20, 2003“That hereafter the Secretary of Commerce is authorized to enter into agreements with one or more nonprofit organizations for the purpose of carrying out collective research and development initiatives pertaining to [former] paragraph (a), and is authorized to seek and accept contributions from public and private sources to support these efforts as necessary.”15 U.S.C. 278kSimilar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriation act:, , .Pub. L. 107–77, title II115 Stat. 774Nov. 28, 2001

Additional Renewal of Federal Financial Assistance for Centers, , , 2681–83, which provided that Federal financial assistance awarded by the Secretary of Commerce to a Regional Center for the Transfer of Manufacturing Technology could continue beyond six years and could be renewed for additional periods, not to exceed one year, at a rate not to exceed one-third of the Center’s total annual costs or the level of funding in the sixth year, whichever was less, subject before any such renewal to a positive evaluation of the Center and to a finding by the Secretary of Commerce that continuation of Federal funding to the Center was in the best interest of the Regional Centers for the Transfer of Manufacturing Technology Program, was from the Departments of Commerce Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1999, and was not repeated in subsequent appropriations Acts. Similar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriation acts:Pub. L. 105–277, div. A, § 101(b) [title II]112 Stat. 2681–50Oct. 21, 1998, , .Pub. L. 105–119, title II111 Stat. 2476Nov. 26, 1997, , , 3009–36.Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title I, § 101(a) [title II]110 Stat. 3009Sept. 30, 1996, , .Pub. L. 103–317, title II108 Stat. 1741Aug. 26, 1994

Publication in Federal Register, , , provided that the requirement of former subsec. (c)(2) of this section was met by the publication made by the National Bureau of Standards on , at 53 F.R. 27060.Pub. L. 100–519, title I, § 102(d)102 Stat. 2590Oct. 24, 1988July 18, 1988

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–358, title VII, § 702124 Stat. 4041Jan. 4, 2011“Congress finds the following:“(1)Over its 20-year existence, the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership has proven its value to manufacturers as demonstrated by the resulting impact on jobs and the economies of all 50 States and the Nation as a whole.“(2)The Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership has helped thousands of companies reinvest in themselves through process improvement and business growth initiatives leading to more sales, new markets, and the adoption of technology to deliver new products and services.“(3)Manufacturing is an increasingly important part of the construction sector as the industry moves to the use of more components and factory built sub-assemblies.“(4)Construction practices must become more efficient and precise if the United States is to construct and renovate its building stock to reduce related carbon emissions to levels that are consistent with combating global warming.“(5)Many companies involved in construction are small, without access to innovative manufacturing techniques, and could benefit from the type of training and business analysis activities that the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership routinely provides to the Nation’s manufacturers and their supply chains.“(6)Broadening the competitiveness grant program under [former] section 25(f) of the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act ([former] , now see ) could help develop and diffuse knowledge necessary to capture a large portion of the estimated $100 billion or more in energy savings if buildings in the United States met the level and quality of energy efficiency now found in buildings in certain other countries.15 U.S.C. 278k(f)15 U.S.C. 278k–1“(7)It is therefore in the national interest to expand the capabilities of the Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership to be supportive of the construction and green energy industries.”

Additional Renewal of Federal Financial Assistance for Centers, , , 2681–83, which provided that Federal financial assistance awarded by the Secretary of Commerce to a Regional Center for the Transfer of Manufacturing Technology could continue beyond six years and could be renewed for additional periods, not to exceed one year, at a rate not to exceed one-third of the Center’s total annual costs or the level of funding in the sixth year, whichever was less, subject before any such renewal to a positive evaluation of the Center and to a finding by the Secretary of Commerce that continuation of Federal funding to the Center was in the best interest of the Regional Centers for the Transfer of Manufacturing Technology Program, was from the Departments of Commerce Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1999, and was not repeated in subsequent appropriations Acts. children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Area career and technical education school
children.label: 2 Area career and technical education school
children.text: The term “area career and technical education school” has the meaning given the term in .section 2302 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: is created under subsection (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: is affiliated with an eligible entity that applies for and is awarded financial support under subsection (e).

children.heading: Community college
children.label: 4 Community college
children.text: The term “community college” means an institution of higher education (as defined under ) at which the highest degree that is predominately awarded to students is an associate’s degree.section 1001(a) of title 20

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 5 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a United States-based nonprofit institution, an institution of higher education, or a State, United States territory, local, or tribal government or a consortium thereof.

children.heading: Historically Black college and university
children.label: 6 Historically Black college and university
children.text: The term “historically Black college and university” has the meaning given the term “part B institution” in .section 1061 of title 20

children.heading: Institution of higher education1So in original. Two pars. (7) have been enacted.1
children.label: 7 Institution of higher education1So in original. Two pars. (7) have been enacted.1
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given such term in .section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership or Program1
children.label: 7 Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership or Program1
children.text: The term “Hollings Manufacturing Extension Partnership” or “Program” means the program established under subsection (b).

children.heading: MEP Advisory Board
children.label: 8 MEP Advisory Board
children.text: The term “MEP Advisory Board” means the Manufacturing Extension Partnership Advisory Board established under subsection (n).2So in original. Probably should refer to subsec. (m).2

children.heading: Minority-serving institution
children.label: 9 Minority-serving institution
children.text: The term “minority-serving institution” means a Hispanic-serving institution as defined in ; an Alaska Native-serving institution or Native Hawaiian-serving institution as defined in ; or a Predominantly Black institution, Asian American and Native American Pacific Islander-serving institution, or Native American-serving nontribal institution as defined in .section 1101a(a) of title 20section 1059d(b) of title 20section 1067q(c) of title 20

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: 10 Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” has the meaning given such term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: Tribal College or University
children.label: 11 Tribal College or University
children.text: The term “Tribal College or University” has the meaning given the term “Tribal College or University” in .section 1059c of title 20

children.heading: Establishment and purpose
children.label: b Establishment and purpose
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director and, if appropriate, through other Federal officials, shall establish a program to provide assistance for the creation and support of manufacturing extension centers for the transfer of manufacturing technology and best business practices.
the transfer of manufacturing technology and techniques developed at the Institute to Centers and, through them, to manufacturing companies throughout the United States;

the participation of individuals from industry, institutions of higher education, State governments, other Federal agencies, and, when appropriate, the Institute in cooperative technology transfer activities;
efforts to make new manufacturing technology and processes usable by United States-based small and medium-sized companies;

the active dissemination of scientific, engineering, technical, and management information about manufacturing to United States-based industrial firms, including small and medium-sized manufacturing companies;
the utilization, when appropriate, of the expertise and capability that exists in Federal agencies, other than the Institute, and federally-sponsored laboratories;

the provision to secondary schools, community colleges, and area career and technical education schools, including those in underserved and rural communities, of information about the job skills needed in manufacturing companies, including small and medium-sized manufacturing businesses in the regions they serve;

the promotion and expansion of certification systems offered through industry, associations, local secondary schools and local colleges, including historically Black colleges and universities, Tribal Colleges or Universities, minority-serving institutions, community colleges, and secondary schools and colleges in underserved and rural communities, when appropriate, including efforts such as facilitating training, supporting new or existing apprenticeships or other applied learning opportunities, and providing access to information and experts, to address workforce needs and skills gaps in order to assist small- and medium-sized manufacturing businesses; and
the growth in employment and wages at United States-based small and medium-sized companies.

the establishment of automated manufacturing systems and other advanced production technologies, at United States-based industrial facilities, including small and medium manufacturing companies based on Institute-supported research, for the purpose of demonstrations and technology transfer;

the active transfer and dissemination of research findings and Center expertise to a wide range of United States-based companies and enterprises, particularly small and medium-sized manufacturers; and

the facilitation of collaborations and partnerships between United States-based small and medium-sized manufacturing companies and local high schools, community colleges, and area career and technical education schools, including those in underserved and rural communities, to help those entities better understand the specific needs of manufacturers and to help manufacturers better understand the skill sets that students learn in the programs offered by such colleges and schools.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: 1 Authorization
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Secretary may provide financial assistance for the creation and support of a Center through a cooperative agreement with an eligible entity.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: 2 Cost sharing
children.text: The Secretary may not provide more than 50 percent of the capital and annual operating and maintenance funds required to establish and support a Center.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2), any amount received by an eligible entity for a Center under a provision of law other than paragraph (1) shall not be considered an amount provided under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 4 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may revise or promulgate such regulations as necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible entity shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the Program;

children.label: B
children.text: the application procedures;

children.label: C
children.text: performance metrics;

children.label: D
children.text: criteria for determining qualified applicants; and3So in original.3

children.label: E
children.text: criteria for choosing recipients of financial assistance from among the qualified applicants.3

children.label: F
children.text: procedures for determining allowable cost share contributions; and

children.label: G
children.text: such other program policy objectives and operational procedures as the Secretary considers necessary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be considered for financial assistance under this section, an applicant shall provide adequate assurances that the applicant and if applicable, the applicant’s partnering organizations, will obtain funding for not less than 50 percent of the capital and annual operating and maintenance funds required to establish and support the Center from sources other than the financial assistance provided under subsection (e).

children.label: i
children.text: is programmatically reasonable;

children.label: ii
children.text: will help accomplish programmatic objectives; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is allocable under Program procedures under subsection (f)(2).

children.heading: Legal rights
children.label: 4 Legal rights
children.text: Each applicant shall include in the application a proposal for the allocation of the legal rights associated with any intellectual property which may result from the activities of the Center.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall subject each application to merit review.

children.label: i
children.text: the merits of the application, particularly those portions of the application regarding technology transfer, training and education, and adaptation of manufacturing technologies to the needs of particular industrial sectors in the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: the quality of service to be provided;

children.label: iii
children.text: the geographical diversity and extent of the service area; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the type and percentage of funding and in-kind commitment from other sources under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Third and eighth year evaluations by panel
children.label: 1 Third and eighth year evaluations by panel

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that each Center is evaluated during its third and eighth years of operation by an evaluation panel appointed by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: private experts, none of whom are connected with the Center evaluated by the panel; and

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal officials.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: C Chairperson
children.text: For each evaluation panel appointed under subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall appoint a chairperson who is an official of the Institute.

children.heading: Fifth year evaluations by Secretary
children.label: 2 Fifth year evaluations by Secretary
children.text: In the fifth year of operation of a Center, the Secretary shall conduct a review of the Center.

children.label: A
children.text: the objective specified in subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: the performance metrics under subsection (f)(2)(C); and

children.label: C
children.text: such other criterion as considered appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an evaluation occurring in the third year of a Center, through the fifth year of the Center;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an evaluation occurring in the fifth year of a Center, through the eighth year of the Center; and

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of an evaluation occurring in the eighth year of a Center, through the tenth year of the Center.

children.heading: Probation
children.label: A Probation
children.text: If an evaluation of a Center is other than positive, the Secretary shall put the Center on probation during the period beginning on the date that the Center receives notice under subparagraph (B)(i) and ending on the date that the reevaluation is complete under subparagraph (B)(iii).

children.label: i
children.text: notify the Center of the reason, including any deficiencies in the performance of the Center identified during the evaluation;

children.label: ii
children.text: assist the Center in remedying the deficiencies by providing the Center, not less frequently than once every 3 months, an analysis of the Center, if considered appropriate by the panel or Secretary, as applicable; and

children.label: iii
children.text: reevaluate the Center not later than 1 year after the date of the notice under clause (i).

children.heading: Continued support during period of probation
children.label: C Continued support during period of probation

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may continue to provide financial assistance under subsection (e) for a Center during the probation period.

children.heading: Post probation
children.label: ii Post probation
children.text: After the period of probation, the Secretary shall not provide any financial assistance unless the Center has received a positive evaluation under subparagraph (B)(iii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a Center fails to remedy a deficiency or to show significant improvement in performance before the end of the probation period under paragraph (5), the Secretary shall conduct a competition to select an operator for the Center under subsection (h).

children.heading: Treatment of Centers subject to new competition
children.label: B Treatment of Centers subject to new competition
children.text: Upon the selection of an operator for a Center under subsection (h), the Center shall be considered a new Center and the calculation of the years of operation of that Center for purposes of paragraphs (1) through (5) of this subsection and subsection (h)(1) shall start anew.

children.heading: Reapplication competition for financial assistance after 10 years
children.label: h Reapplication competition for financial assistance after 10 years

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If an eligible entity has operated a Center under this section for a period of 10 consecutive years, the Secretary shall conduct a competition to select an eligible entity to operate the Center in accordance with the process plan under subsection (i).

children.heading: Incumbent eligible entities
children.label: 2 Incumbent eligible entities
children.text: An eligible entity that has received financial assistance under this section for a period of 10 consecutive years and that the Secretary determines is in good standing shall be eligible to compete in the competition under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Treatment of Centers subject to reapplication competition
children.label: 3 Treatment of Centers subject to reapplication competition
children.text: Upon the selection of an operator for a Center under paragraph (1), the Center shall be considered a new Center and the calculation of the years of operation of that Center for purposes of paragraphs (1) through (5) of subsection (g) shall start anew.

children.heading: Process plan
children.label: i Process plan
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall implement and submit to Congress a plan for how the Institute will conduct an evaluation, competition, and reapplication competition under this section.January 6, 2017

children.heading: Protection of confidential information of Center clients
children.label: 1 Protection of confidential information of Center clients
A
children.text: Information on the business operation of any participant in the Program or of a client of a Center.

children.label: B
children.text: Trade secrets of any client of a Center.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: As a condition on receipt of financial assistance for a Center under subsection (e), an eligible entity shall establish a board to oversee the operations of the Center.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director shall establish appropriate standards for each board described under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Considerations
children.label: B Considerations
children.text: In establishing the standards, the Director shall take into account the type and organizational structure of an eligible entity.

children.label: i
children.text: membership;

children.label: ii
children.text: composition;

children.label: iii
children.text: term limits;

children.label: iv
children.text: conflicts of interest; and

children.label: v
children.text: such other requirements as the Director considers necessary.

children.label: i
children.text: The membership of each board shall be representative of stakeholders in the region in which the Center is located.

children.label: ii
children.text: A majority of the members of the board shall be selected from among individuals who own or are employed by small or medium-sized manufacturers.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: A member of a board established under paragraph (1) may not serve on more than 1 board established under that paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each board established under paragraph (1) shall adopt and submit to the Director bylaws to govern the operation of the board.

children.heading: Conflicts of interest
children.label: B Conflicts of interest
children.text: Bylaws adopted under subparagraph (A) shall include policies to minimize conflicts of interest, including such policies relating to disclosure of relationships and recusal as may be necessary to minimize conflicts of interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To the extent provided in advance in appropriations Acts, other Federal departments and agencies may transfer amounts to the Institute, and the Secretary and Director may accept and make available cash donations from the private sector pursuant to , to be used for strengthening United States manufacturing under this section.section 272(c)(7) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the manufacturing market or sector targeted is limited geographically or in scope;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of States (or territory, in the case of Puerto Rico) with Centers serving manufacturers of such market or sector is five or fewer; and

children.label: C
children.text: such Center has or Centers have received a positive evaluation in the most recent evaluation conducted pursuant to subsection (g).

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established within the Institute a Manufacturing Extension Partnership Advisory Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The MEP Advisory Board shall consist of not fewer than 10 members appointed by the Director and broadly representative of stakeholders.

children.label: I
children.text: at least 2 members shall be employed by or on an advisory board for a Center;

children.label: II
children.text: at least 5 members shall be from United States small businesses in the manufacturing sector; and

children.label: III
children.text: at least 1 member shall represent a community college.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iii Limitation
children.text: No member of the MEP Advisory Board shall be an employee of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Term
children.label: B Term
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), the term of office of each member of the MEP Advisory Board shall be 3 years.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: C Vacancies
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring prior to the expiration of the term for which his predecessor was appointed shall be appointed for the remainder of such term.

children.heading: Serving consecutive terms
children.label: D Serving consecutive terms
children.text: Any person who has completed 2 consecutive full terms of service on the MEP Advisory Board shall thereafter be ineligible for appointment during the 1-year period following the expiration of the second such term.

children.label: A
children.text: meet not less than biannually; and

children.label: i
children.text: advice on the activities, plans, and policies of the Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: assessments of the soundness of the plans and strategies of the Program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: assessments of current performance against the plans of the Program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In discharging its duties under this subsection, the MEP Advisory Board shall function solely in an advisory capacity, in accordance with chapter 10 of title 5.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: shall not apply to the MEP Advisory Board.Section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: At a minimum, the MEP Advisory Board shall transmit an annual report to the Secretary for transmittal to Congress not later than 30 days after the submission to Congress of the President’s annual budget under .section 1105 of title 31

children.heading: Contents
children.label: B Contents
children.text: The report shall address the status of the Program and describe the relevant sections of the programmatic planning document and updates thereto transmitted to Congress by the Director under subsections (c) and (d) of section 278i of this title.4See References in Text note below.4

children.label: A
children.text: identify obstacles that prevent United States-based small manufacturers from effectively competing in the global market;

children.label: B
children.text: implement a comprehensive plan to train the Centers to address the obstacles identified in paragraph (2); and

children.label: C
children.text: facilitate improved communication between the Centers to assist such manufacturers in implementing appropriate, targeted solutions to the obstacles identified in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Development of open access resources
children.label: 2 Development of open access resources
children.text: As part of the Program, the Secretary shall develop open access resources that address best practices related to inventory sourcing, supply chain management, manufacturing techniques, available Federal resources, and other topics to further the competitiveness and profitability of small manufacturers.
